100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On July 21, 1926, the British government announced the dissolution of the coal miners' strike, bringing an end to a labor dispute that had gripped the nation for weeks. In the United States, the Senate continued discussions on the controversial tariff bill, which aims to protect domestic industries amid rising global trade tensions. Meanwhile, tensions in the Middle East escalated as the British mandate in Palestine faced increasing unrest from local Arab populations.
Updated: Tuesday, July 21, 2026 at 08:06:24 AM CDT
